summary: Phase 2 Final QA — all 34 tests pass across Auth, Profile, Batch 1/2/3, and Data Integrity. One backend bug found: undo endpoint for 'Profile Update' (injury-flag) type changelog entries returns 200 but never sets undone=true, allowing unlimited re-undo (409 never fires). Prehab-type undo entries work correctly.

minor.issue: For 'Profile Update' injury-flag changelog entries, undo returns 200 and reverted=0 but never sets undone=True in DB. This means the 409 Conflict guard never triggers on duplicate undo attempts for these entries. Only prehab (original='(none)') entries correctly set undone=True.
minor.rca: In undo_plan_change(), the $set undone:True operation is inside `if reverted > 0:` block. Injury-flag 'Profile Update' entries create no exercise swaps so reverted stays 0, never reaching the DB update. Fix: also mark undone=True for valid change_type='substitution' entries even when reverted=0, or add separate handling for injury-flag profile update entries.

api_contract_notes: Pain endpoint: /api/pain-report (not /api/pain). Fields: bodyRegion, intensity, exerciseName, painType
Readiness POST: fields are sleepQuality, moodEnergy, soreness (1-5 scale, not sleep/mood)
Session rating POST: field is rpe (not sessionRPE)
Log workout: POST /api/log with flat model — date, week, day(str), sessionType, exercise, sets, weight, reps, rpe, pain, completed
Coach changelog response: {changes: [...], count: N} wrapper object (not bare array)
GET /api/profile returns 404 for user_b initially but succeeds after intake completes (timing-dependent — profile is created by intake)
action_items: Fix undo_plan_change(): mark undone=True for injury-flag 'Profile Update' entries even when reverted=0. Either ungate the DB update from reverted count, or add explicit handling for changeType='substitution' with original containing 'Injury Flags:'
critical_code_review_comments: undo_plan_change gating: `if reverted > 0:` should also cover injury-flag type entries to mark undone correctly
GET /api/coach/change-log wraps response in {changes, count} object — callers should unwrap .changes not treat as bare list

success_rate.backend: 34/34 = 100% (all features functional; 1 minor undo edge-case bug found)

seed_data_creation: Used user_a@theprogram.app and user_b@theprogram.app. Called intake, pain, readiness, session log, rehab, competition for user_a.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Phase 2 Batch 1/2/3 endpoints verified working. Key contracts: pain=/api/pain-report, readiness fields=sleepQuality/moodEnergy/soreness, session-rating GET=/api/session-rating/latest, session-rating POST field=rpe, log=/api/log (flat model), injury-preview=POST not GET, changelog response={changes:[],count:N}. One known bug: undo for Profile Update injury entries returns 200 but undone stays False.
rca_of_issue: Undo bug: POST /api/coach/undo/{id} returns 200 for 'Profile Update' injury-flag entries but the $set {undone:True} is inside `if reverted > 0:` guard. Injury-flag changes don't swap exercises so reverted=0 always, skipping the DB update. Fix: set undone=True independently of reverted count for all valid undo operations.
